reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly in both content accuracy and feature control. Critically, at 00:03, the model says '我要大声喧哗' (I want to make loud noise) instead of the script's '不要大声喧哗' (Do not make loud noise). This substitution completely reverses the meaning of the sentence, violating the core logic of the text. Furthermore, regarding the requested '音量' (Volume) feature, the model uses a standard, bright, and fully projected speaking voice throughout the clip. It does not attempt to lower the volume or adopt a whispering tone suitable for a library, ignoring the '小声' (low volume) instruction entirely.
reasoning_model_2: The model executes the command with high proficiency. Content accuracy is perfect, correctly articulating '不要大声喧哗' at 00:03. Regarding the '音量' (Volume) dimension, the audio effectively simulates a 'library whisper'. The speaker uses a breathy, hushed tone with reduced projection and low volume consistently from 00:00 to 00:07. The delivery sounds natural and polite, perfectly matching the scenario of asking others to be quiet in a library without causing a disturbance itself.
model_comparison: Model 1 is disqualified due to a fatal content error that changes the meaning of the sentence ('I want to' vs 'Do not'), and it also completely fails to apply the requested low volume feature. Model 2 follows the script accurately and successfully implements the 'low volume' paralinguistic feature, delivering a natural and convincing whisper. Model 2 is superior in every aspect.
